summary: Iteration 2 of Arrow Maze fully verified end-to-end on mobile viewport (375x667). Backend: all 10 pytest cases pass (health, idempotent POST /players, score create/sort/no _id, global+per-level leaderboards, stats aggregation, 404s). Frontend: new home menu renders brand 'Arrows', LEVEL 1 (after fresh state), win streak + cleared meta, Continue button, Daily+Awards cards, All-levels button, and the three icon shortcuts (daily/awards/settings). Settings screen exposes all 5 rows (Language English read-only, Vibrations, Sounds, Dark Mode, Native Refresh Rate); toggling Dark Mode actually flips the entire app to dark theme (verified via screenshot showing dark navy background + white text) and toggling back returns to light. Daily screen renders monthly progress card with trophy + day grid (30 tiles for the current month). Awards screen renders 4 stat cards (longest day streak, highest win streak, most wins, highest level) and a 12-cell monthly trophy grid (locked icons in default state). Levels screen shows 10 numeric tiles + Infinite tile. Gameplay: Level 1 was brute-force solved in exactly 3 moves with MOVES incrementing only on successful clears, then the victory overlay appeared with rotated word 'Excellent!' (text testid victory-text), TIME/MOVES stats, and Next/Menu buttons; clearing level 1 advanced the home LEVEL display to 2 (persisted via local stats). On Level 5 (10 arrows) the bounce-back path was confirmed: 3 different arrows when tapped did NOT increment MOVES and remained on the board, while the other 7 cleared normally — exactly the requested 'tap-blocked = bounce, no MOVES change, no removal' behaviour. Daily deep-link /game/daily-2026-06-27 renders header 'DAILY · 2026-06-27'. Infinite mode renders 'INFINITE · ROUND 1' with an 8-arrow procedural board. No critical bugs found. Two non-blocking console warnings: (a) carry-over 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' from victory overlay (already flagged in iteration 1), and (b) new Reanimated warning 'Property [opacity] may be overwritten by a layout animation' on the victory/confetti view.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning still emits: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Move pointerEvents from props into the style object on the Animated.View overlay. Reference: /app/frontend/app/game/[id].tsx (victory Animated.View).

Console warning: '[Reanimated] Property [opacity] may be overwritten by a layout animation. Please wrap your component with an animated view and apply the layout animation on the wrapper.' Fires when the victory overlay mounts. Wrap the inner animated element so layout animation and opacity animation are not applied to the same component. Reference: /app/frontend/app/game/[id].tsx and/or /app/frontend/src/components/Confetti.tsx.

action_items: Move pointerEvents="auto" from prop to style on the victory Animated.View (silences RN-Web deprecation warning).
Wrap the opacity-animated victory/confetti view with a separate Animated wrapper so Reanimated stops warning about layout animation overriding opacity.
Optional cleanup: the global leaderboard contains TEST_-prefixed entries from previous pytest runs; safe to leave or purge later.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py remains clean: async motor queries, _id excluded with both projection {_id:0} and _serialize helper, idempotent POST /players, Pydantic validation on PlayerCreate.
Frontend api calls use process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL via src/game/api.ts (no hardcoded URLs).
All interactive elements expose testID. Text is wrapped in <Text>. StyleSheet.create used. SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context used on every screen.
Dark Mode propagates through useColors() hook driven by SettingsProvider — verified visually: full background + chrome flip on toggle.
settings.tsx (~227 lines), index.tsx (~360 lines), daily.tsx (~266 lines), awards.tsx (~253 lines), levels.tsx (~213 lines): all under the 700-line threshold.
seed_data_creation: Pytest run added new TEST_-prefixed players and scores to MongoDB (idempotent + scoped to TEST_LVL_<uuid> level ids). Local browser storage on the preview also has stats.currentLevel=2 after the Level-1 clear during the UI run; the main agent may reset localStorage if a fresh state is needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App URL https://maze-flow-play.preview.emergentagent.com, backend prefix /api. Run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_arrow_maze_api.py -v. Bounce verification: navigate to /game/5 — at least 3 arrows are blocked on the seeded board and will visibly bounce (MOVES counter stays put, tile remains). Victory word is randomly chosen from [Spectacular/Superb/Brilliant/Excellent/Fantastic]; test should accept any of them. Daily route format /game/daily-YYYY-MM-DD; infinite route /game/infinite. The DOM under Expo Router web keeps prior screens mounted — always use .first when selecting common testIDs like brand-mark or game-header-label.
rca_of_issue: No bugs to root-cause this iteration; both warnings are non-functional and have suggested fixes listed in action_items.
